vocabularies.definition: the practice of protecting computers, networks, and personal data from being stolen, changed, or damaged

objectives.description: Describe how failures may stem from poor design, user error, or technical breakdowns
Identify ways digital systems can fail and impact users' daily lives
Reflect on how failures affect different users emotionally and functionally

vocabularies.definition: software intended to damage a computing system or to take partial control over its operation
any potential cause of harm to a computer system or network
a weakness in a system that could be exploited by an attacker

objectives.description: Describe system-level threats such as malware, ransomware, weak default settings, or misconfigured file sharing.
Explain how AI may increase the speed or scale of these threats.
Explain how attackers exploit technical vulnerabilities to gain control, disrupt access, or corrupt data.

vocabularies.definition: a technique that attempts to trick a user into providing personal information. That personal information can then be used to access sensitive online resources, such as bank accounts and emails
a malicious activity aimed at disrupting, damaging, destroying or gaining unauthorized access to information systems, networks or the data they contain

objectives.description: Analyze how phishing and password attacks exploit human behavior -  such as trust, emotion, and predictability
Investigate identity-based cyber threats by analyzing phishing messages and password-related breaches

vocabularies.definition: a video or audio that has been manipulated using AI to look or sound like a real person
a type of phishing that happens over text message (SMS)
manipulating people into giving up private information or doing something risky
a type of phishing that happens over phone call or voicemail

objectives.description: Compare basic strategies for prevention
Examine how attackers use deception to manipulate people into revealing sensitive information or taking risky actions
Explain how AI tools (e.g., generative text, deepfake video, voice cloning, chatbot mimicry) increase the realism, reach, and personalization of social engineering attacks
Evaluate different defenses to determine what made them effective
Explain how system design, human behavior, and AI can work together to prevent a cyber threat

vocabularies.definition: a set of core rules for protecting information and stands for Confidentiality, Integrity, and Availability
making information accessible to the people who need it, when they need it
keeping information private so only the right people can see it
ensuring that information stays correct and hasn't been changed without permission

objectives.description: Describe how a system vulnerability allowed a confidentiality, integrity, or availability failure and propose a user-centered fix
Identify cybersecurity failures based on their impact on confidentiality, integrity, or availability (CIA Triad)

vocabularies.definition: a set of tools used in cybersecurity to help systems protect information and manage what users can do
when a system keeps track of what users do so suspicious activity can be detected and reviewed
the process a system uses to check that you are who you say you are
rules a system uses to decide what you're allowed to do once you're logged in

objectives.description: Apply authentication and access control models to real-world scenarios
Explain how systems verify identity and manage access to information
Analyze how AI supports security features like fraud detection, anomaly detection, and adaptive authentication
Explain how AI is used in threat detection to identify suspicious patterns

vocabularies.definition: a process that reverses encryption, taking a secret message and reproducing the original plain text
a process of encoding messages to keep them secret, so only "authorized" parties can read it
encryption that uses two different keys: a public key to lock the message and a private key to unlock it
encryption where the same key is used to lock and unlock the message

objectives.description: Apply encryption concepts to different data states (at rest, in transit, in use)
Describe how encryption helps protect data
Differentiate between symmetric and asymmetric encryption
Analyze a tradeoff introduced by AI's role in encryption
Identify ways AI can both weaken and strengthen encryption
Model how AI and computational power influence the effectiveness of encryption systems
Describe how a security feature might unintentionally create a barrier for users
Redesign a security feature to better balance security and usability

vocabularies.definition: using multiple, independent security features to protect systems and data

objectives.description: Apply computational thinking to analyze cyberattacks, identify system vulnerabilities, and reason through defense strategies
Design a layered defense that balances protection, usability, and constraints
Evaluate design choices in system features that balance security and usability, including those enhanced by AI
Design secure system improvements that balance protection with usability, using trade-off reasoning
Explain how solutions work and how they help real users
Identify security and usability issues in a digital system by analyzing user needs and risks
